context: Tests whether the agent builds the social proof API endpoint following the skill's specific patterns: a unified endpoint using parallel DB/Redis queries, correct PII anonymization, Redis sorted-set visitor tracking with the right window and key expiry, the correct isLow threshold, the Math.max floor for activeVisitors, and the random boost for very low counts.

checklist.name: Unified endpoint path
checklist.description: The route handler is for GET /api/products/:id/social-proof (or equivalent parameterised path with the id segment)

checklist.name: Parallel data fetching
checklist.description: The handler uses Promise.all (or equivalent concurrent await) to fetch recentOrders/purchases, activeVisitors, reviewSummary, and stockLevel in parallel rather than sequentially

checklist.name: PII anonymization — fields
checklist.description: Each purchase entry in the response contains only firstName and a city/state or city/country location string — no full names, email addresses, or order IDs

checklist.name: PII anonymization — no order IDs
checklist.description: The response/design-notes explicitly states that order IDs are NOT included in purchase notification data

checklist.name: Redis sorted-set visitor tracking
checklist.description: The trackProductPageView function uses redis.zadd to record session presence and redis.zremrangebyscore to prune stale entries

checklist.name: 5-minute sliding window
checklist.description: The visitor tracking uses a 5-minute window (300,000 ms or 5 * 60 * 1000) for active visitor detection

checklist.name: Redis key expiry 600s
checklist.description: redis.expire is called on the visitor key with 600 seconds

checklist.name: Math.max floor for activeVisitors
checklist.description: The API response applies Math.max(count, 1) (or equivalent) so activeVisitors is never returned as 0

checklist.name: Random boost for low counts
checklist.description: When the raw visitor count is below 3, a small random offset (e.g. Math.floor(Math.random() * 3)) is added to the count

checklist.name: isLow threshold
checklist.description: The isLow flag in the stock level response is true when stockLevel > 0 AND stockLevel <= 5 (not 3, not 10 — specifically 5)

checklist.name: 24-hour purchase window
checklist.description: Recent orders are fetched using a 24-hour lookback window (last 24h from now)

checklist.name: Async loading noted
checklist.description: design-notes.md mentions that the social proof data should be loaded asynchronously / after page load, not blocking the critical rendering path

checklist.name: timeAgo included
checklist.description: Each purchase entry includes a timeAgo field (human-readable elapsed time) rather than a raw timestamp
